---
title: "graphs"
format: html
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

## Paleo

### Zachos_2010

```{r}
isotopes <- read_tsv("../Data/Paleo/Zachos et al. 2010/208-1262_isotope.tab", skip = 20)
bulk_stable <- read_tsv("../Data/Paleo/Zachos et al. 2010/208-1262_bulk_stable_isotope.tab", skip = 23)
age_model <- read_tsv("../Data/Paleo/Zachos et al. 2010/207-1258_208-1262_age_model.tab", skip = 32)
zachos <- read_tsv("../Data/Paleo/Zachos et al. 2010/Zachos_2010.tab", skip = 23)
```


Bulk stable is what we want: `isotopes` extends to older ages, which we don't care about. `Zachos` is data only from Zachos, whereas `isotopes` also includes other papers.

```{r}
bulk_stable <- bulk_stable%>%
  mutate(delta_c_13 = `δ13C [‰ PDB]`,
         delta_o_18 = `δ18O [‰]`)

bulk_stable%>%
  # filter( `Age [ka BP]` > 55350 & `Age [ka BP]` < 55600)%>%
  ggplot(aes(x = `Age [ka BP]`, y = delta_c_13))+
  geom_line()+
  theme_minimal()

bulk_stable%>%
  ggplot(aes(x = `Age [ka BP]`, y = delta_o_18))+
  geom_line()+
  theme_minimal()

```

## Modern

```{r}
library(ncdf4)
temp_ocean <- read_csv("../Data/Modern/avg_temp_anomaly_ocean.csv", skip = 3)

temp_ocean <- temp_ocean%>%
  mutate(Date = str_c(str_extract(Date, "\\d{4}"), str_extract(Date, "(?<=\\d{4}).*"), sep = "-"),
         Date = ym(Date))

temp_land <- read_csv("../Data/Modern/avg_temp_anomaly_land.csv", skip = 3)

temp_land <- temp_land%>%
  mutate(Date = str_c(str_extract(Date, "\\d{4}"), str_extract(Date, "(?<=\\d{4}).*"), sep = "-"),
         Date = ym(Date))

co2 <- read_csv("../Data/Modern/co2_annmean_mlo.csv", skip = 43)
```
